data: Marble plaque damaged on the upper right corner and lower left corner, set in plaster in modern times.
Irregular letters: angular Ε, lunate Σ, cursive Ω. A with a slanted bar (except at l. 1 where the bar is straight), Υ with the form of the Latin V. Interpuncts divide numerals from words.
Observed by Amico and Castelli in Museo Biscari, later by Korhonen in magazzino del cortile, Collezione Biscari (formerly sala VI 96).
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana
Castello Ursino, Piazza Federico II di Svevia, 95121 Catania (CT)
V. and Statella Amico E., Catana Ilustrata, vol. 3 (Catanae, 1741)

G. Kaibel, Inscriptiones Graecae Siciliae et Italiae, additis graecis Galliae Hispaniae, Britanniae, Germaniae inscriptionibus, Inscriptiones Graecae consilio et auctoritate Academiae Litterarum Regiae Borussicae Editae. Volumen XIV., XIV (Berlin: Georgius Reimerus, 1890)

Kalle Korhonen, Le iscrizioni del Museo civico di Catania : storia delle collezioni, cultura epigrafica, edizione (Helsinki: Societas Scientiarum Fennica, 2004)

Text encoding: Marta Fogagnolo, Alex Antoniou
1Ἐνθάδε
2κεῖται Οὔρ-
3σουλος ζή-
4[σα]ς ἔτη #interpunct κη´ μῆ-
5[νας] ς´ #interpunct ἡμέρ(ας) #stop η´ #interpunct τελ-
6[ευτᾷ] καλ(ανδαῖς) Αὐγ(ούσταις)

Text encoding: Dmitry Dundua, Robert Crellin
Ἐνθάδε κεῖται Οὔρσουλος ζήσας ἔτη κη μῆνας ς ἡμέρας η τελευτᾷ καλανδαῖς Αὐγούσταις

Text encoding: Marta Fogagnolo, Alex Antoniou
1ΕΝΘΑΔΕ
2ΚΕΙΤΑΙΟΥΡ
3ΣΟΥΛΟΣΖΗ
4[..]ΣΕΤΗ()ΚΗΜΗ
5[...]Σ()ΗΜΕΡ()Η()ΤΕΛ
6[......]ΚΑΛΑΥΓ

Text encoding: Dmitry Dundua, Robert Crellin
ΕΝΘΑΔΕΚΕΙΤΑΙΟΥΡΣΟΥΛΟΣΖΗΣΑΣΕΤΗΚΗΜΗΝΑΣΣΗΜΕΡΑΣΗΤΕΛΕΥΤΑΚΑΛΑΝΔΑΙΣΑΥΓΟΥΣΤΑΙΣ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text based on photograph ;
  • lines.2-3: Kirchhoff: Οὐρσοῦλος; Kaibel, Wessel, Korhonen: Οὔρ|σουλος
  • line.4: Amico, Kirchhoff, Kaibel, Wessel: κε; Torremuzza, Ferrara, Korhonen: κη
  • lines.5-6: Ferrara: τελ[εύτατος] (sic); Kirchhoff: τελ[ευτᾷ---]; Kaibel: τελ(ευτήσας); Korhonen: τελ|[ευτᾷ]
  • line.6: Kirchhoff: καλ(ανδ---) Αὐγ[ούστ---]; Kaibel: [τῇ πρὸ ---] καλ(ανδῶν) Αὐγ(ούστων); Wessel: [πρὸ ---] καλ. Αὐγ.; Korhonen: καλ(ανδαῖς) Αὐγ(ούσταις). [τῇ πρ(ὸ) ---] καλ(ανδῶν) Αὐγ(ούστων) seems less probable because of the space left, considering that the verb τελευτάω is never abbreviated in inscriptions from Catania

English translation

Here lies Oursoulos, having lived for 28 years, 6 months and 8 days. He died on the Kalends of August.

Italian translation

Marta Fogagnolo

Qui giace Oursoulos, che ha vissuto per 28 anni, 6 mesi e 8 giorni. È morto nelle Calende di agosto.

commentary

This inscription presents the traditional structure of the Christian funerary inscriptions from Catania, with the formula ἐνθάδε κεῖται ὁ δεῖνα, the age of the deceased with the participle of ζάω and a second sentence introduced by τελευτᾷ (which never appears in the participle form: for this reason, the restoration of Kaibel, τελευτήσας, has to be rejected) with the date of death, dies natalis for the Christians. The name of the deceased, Οὔρσουλος, recurs in other inscriptions from the imperial age (IGB 3.1772).

[τῇ πρ(ὸ) ---] καλ(ανδῶν) Αὐγ(ούστων) seems less probable because of the space left, considering that the verb τελευτάω is never abbreviated in inscriptions from Catania

Here lies Oursoulos, having lived for 28 years, 6 months and 8 days. He died on the Kalends of August.

Qui giace Oursoulos, che ha vissuto per 28 anni, 6 mesi e 8 giorni. È morto nelle Calende di agosto.

This inscription presents the traditional structure of the Christian funerary inscriptions from Catania, with the formula ἐνθάδε κεῖται ὁ δεῖνα, the age of the deceased with the participle of ζάω and a second sentence introduced by τελευτᾷ (which never appears in the participle form: for this reason, the restoration of Kaibel, τελευτήσας, has to be rejected) with the date of death, dies natalis for the Christians. The name of the deceased, Οὔρσουλος, recurs in other inscriptions from the imperial age (IGB 3.1772).
